items.title: Major disturbance unfolds at Perth's maximum security prison

children.content: A prison officer has been injured in a major disturbance that caused significant damage at Perth’s maximum-security prison.

gallery.altText: The front façade of a prison at dusk

thumbnail.altText: The front façade of a prison at dusk

items.title: Stink bomb attack planners taken to court by energy giant Woodside

image.altText: Kristen and Joana stand side by side outside court while looking straight at the camera

children.content: Energy giant Woodside has taken climate activists to court in a bid to unmask the conspirators behind a stink-bomb protest that forced the evacuation of its Perth headquarters.

items.title: Carnival atmosphere as BHP staff down tools for landmark strike

children.content: Workers at the mining giant's operations in Port Hedland have walked off the job in one of the most significant industrial disputes to hit the resources sector in a quarter of a century.

articles.title: BHP hits production record prior to landmark industrial action

children.content: As hundreds of BHP workers prepare to down tools in a landmark strike, the miner has revealed its highest-ever levels of iron ore production.

image.altText: An image of iron ore operations including loaders and conveyer belts

gallery.altText: Workers on strike out side of BHP's Port Hedland facilities.
Workers leaving Port Hedland's BHP operations for strike action.
BHP staff walk off the job in Port Hedland for an eight-hour strike.

thumbnail.altText: Workers on strike out side of BHP's Port Hedland facilities.
Workers leaving Port Hedland's BHP operations for strike action.
BHP staff walk off the job in Port Hedland for an eight-hour strike.

items.title: Scars of alleged Invasion Day terror attack run deep six months on

children.content: A day of evidence reflecting on racism, hate and violence directed towards Aboriginal people highlights the ongoing impact of what police described as a potential "mass-casualty event" at a Perth rally.

image.altText: About a dozen police vehicles parked across a road to close it off.

items.title: AusAlerts could compromise 'safe phones' for women at DV risk

children.content: Regional women experiencing domestic violence are at high risk of their safe phones being discovered during tests of the new AusAlert disaster warning system.

image.altText: A phone notification with the words 'Priority AusAlert'.

items.title: Seven-kilometre artwork creates colour journey through Perth suburbs

children.content: Under the raised railway that runs through Perth's south-east is a public art project that took five months to complete and required 2,000 litres of paint.

gallery.altText: Coloured piers and playgrounds near Oats St station
A painted abutment at Cannington Station.

thumbnail.altText: Coloured piers and playgrounds near Oats St station
A painted abutment at Cannington Station.

items.title: Plumber's citizen's arrest of alleged car thief goes viral after handbrake blunder

children.content: A Perth plumber has gone viral on social media after chasing down and restraining an alleged car thief after a botched getaway, but a prominent lawyer warns citizens' arrests are risky.

gallery.altText: A man with someone in a headlock

thumbnail.altText: A man with someone in a headlock

items.title: Transgender woman's 'humiliating' experience with major corporations

children.content: What should have been a quick process of updating personal details left Laura Mitchell "insulted" and "humiliated" by the country's largest institutions.

image.altText: Woman with brown hair looks out at the water while standing at Frankston Pier.

items.title: Cats stretching the lead over Saints at Kardinia

children.content: Geelong hosts St Kilda at Kardinia Park to kick off round 19 of the AFL, with the under-strength Cats desperate to stop a recent slide. Follow live.

image.altText: A smiling Geelong AFL player hugs a goalscoring teammate with his back to camera.

items.title: England's hopes fade again, but it won't stop them dreaming

children.content: England aren't going to be bringing the World Cup home, 60 years of hurt due to extend at least another four years after the devastation of another semifinal defeat.

articles.title: Death-defying Argentina living every World Cup game as if it's the last

children.content: Argentina has been the daredevils of this World Cup, and after another incredible comeback to defeat England in the semifinals, its heady mix of emotion and skill, might just deliver back-to-back titles.

image.altText: Two female Argentina fans cry in celebration after a win

articles.title: Quick hits: Tuchel's tactics allow Messi to orchestrate England crash

children.content: England's World Cup ends in heartbreak again, with questions being asked of its manager's decisions as Lionel Messi plotted Argentina's win and the Three Lions' demise. Here are the quick hits.

image.altText: Harry Kane, Enzo Fernandez, Thomas Tuchel

articles.title: The story behind Messi's photo with a future Spanish superstar

children.content: On Monday morning, Argentinian legend Lionel Messi will go up against rising Spanish superstar Lamine Yamal in the World Cup final, 19 years after their famous photo shoot.

image.altText: A man holds a baby in a towel.

items.title: Why Australians shouldn't panic about fuel prices as war in Iran escalates again

children.content: With the price of oil rising and the fuel excise cut due to end, drivers are facing twin threats when it comes to the price of fuel, but experts say Australia is in a much better position compared with when the Middle East conflict began.

image.altText: Close up of BP petrol station fuel names, Premium 98, Premium 95, E10, Diesel.

items.title: Australian brand has a 'Trump account' after six-figure tariff refund

children.content: Businesses globally are clawing back tens of billions of dollars paid to United States customs after Donald Trump's so-called liberation day tariffs were deemed unlawful.

image.altText: a man in a warehouse smiling

items.title: Second case of deadly bird flu detected in NSW

children.content: A second petrel found on the NSW Mid North Coast has tested positive for H5 bird flu.

image.altText: A large wing-span white bird soars against massive blue ocean waves.

items.title: Sam Neill's cause of death revealed by agent

children.content: New Zealand actor Sam Neill died from pneumonia, his long-time agent Philip Grenz has confirmed.

image.altText: Sam Neill wears a scarf around his neck and jacket. Behind are black and white images of him over the years

items.title: Man's death in Bali leaves trail of debt, scores of customers in limbo

children.content: Cameron Hughes, 39, died in a Balinese immigration detention centre last week under yet-to-be-confirmed circumstances. The ABC can reveal the Perth businessman left behind hundreds of thousands of dollars in unfinished business, with dozens of customers potentially out of pocket.

Cameron standing in the backyard of a home with a combative expression.

Cameron standing in the backyard of a home with a combative expression.

items.title: You'll now be able to spot an AI-generated song on Spotify

children.content: Streaming giant Spotify has stopped short of expunging artificially generated music from its platform, but it is making it easier for listeners to work out that they are listening to tracks produced using AI.

image.altText: Sam Duboff wearing a green shirt standing in front of a Spotify sign.

items.title: Cyclist bitten by brown snake caught in her bike chain

children.content: The woman, in her 60s, was riding on a northern NSW rail trail when she ran over a two-metre snake that became trapped then bit her.

image.altText: snake in bike chain

items.title: 'No white flag' in by-election race with One Nation, Liberal leader says

children.content: WA's Liberal leader says "there is no white flag being waved" in the upcoming Secret Harbour by-election, but states any result other than Labor retaining the seat, namely a win by One Nation, will be a "victory" for the party.

items.title: Boy, 5, dies after being hit by 4WD in suburban Perth

items.title: Q-Day is coming and it might break the entire internet

children.content: The countdown is on to Q-Day, when an algorithm written more than 30 years ago could run on a quantum computer and compromise the security of data on the internet. How are we preparing?

image.altText: Woman with laptop holding her bank card while typing. Close up of computer and hands only.

items.title: Scale of Australia's shortage of child psychiatrists revealed in new report

children.content: Experts are warning younger children are presenting with increasingly complex mental health needs as a new report predicts a nationwide shortfall of child and adolescent psychiatrists.

image.altText: A young woman with dark shoulder-length hair look straight to camera.

items.title: The Odyssey may be the epic we deserve, but not the one we need

children.content: The scope of Christopher Nolan's latest epic might be awesome but a menagerie of supporting faces and questionable characterisations muddle the director's vision.

items.title: Feral deer almost eradicated around two towns despite 'gunship' cull controversy

children.content: After a second annual aerial cull sparked controversy in southern WA, hunters say targeting feral deer from the ground is a more humane option.
